subtopic: Patterns of Distributed Systems — Unmesh Joshi

source.title: Catalog of Patterns of Distributed Systems
source.author: Unmesh Joshi

framing: Pattern catalog covering the implementation-level mechanisms used inside distributed databases, brokers, and coordination systems (Kafka, ZooKeeper, Raft/Paxos impls, Cassandra, HBase, etc.). Strong overlap in spirit with Kleppmann's DDIA but formatted as named patterns. Lower-level than the Azure/microservices.io catalogs.

patterns.name: Clock-Bound Wait
patterns.summary: Wait out clock uncertainty before read/write so values can be globally ordered (Spanner TrueTime style).

patterns.name: Consistent Core
patterns.summary: Run a small strongly-consistent control plane alongside a larger eventually-consistent data plane (HBase + ZK; Kafka + KRaft).

patterns.name: Emergent Leader
patterns.summary: Pick leader implicitly by age in cluster rather than running an explicit election (SWIM style).

patterns.name: Fixed Partitions
patterns.summary: Number of partitions fixed at creation; rebalancing reassigns whole partitions to nodes (Kafka, Elasticsearch).

patterns.name: Follower Reads
patterns.summary: Serve reads from followers for higher throughput, lower latency — tolerating bounded staleness.

patterns.name: Generation Clock
patterns.summary: Monotonic epoch/term number that increments on leadership change; lets followers reject stale leaders (Raft term, Kafka epoch).

patterns.name: Gossip Dissemination
patterns.summary: Each node periodically exchanges state with random peers; information propagates epidemically (Cassandra, Consul SWIM).

patterns.name: HeartBeat
patterns.summary: Periodic 'I'm alive' message from one node to others; absence signals failure. Push-based, distinct from pull-based health checks.

patterns.name: High-Water Mark
patterns.summary: Offset in the replicated log up to which all replicas have ack'd; consumers can only read up to this point (Kafka).

patterns.name: Hybrid Clock
patterns.summary: Combine physical timestamp with logical counter to get monotonic + roughly-real-time ordering (HLC; CockroachDB, YugabyteDB).

patterns.name: Idempotent Receiver
patterns.summary: Server stores request IDs and ignores duplicates so client retries are safe.

patterns.name: Key-Range Partitions
patterns.summary: Partition by sorted key ranges for efficient range scans (HBase, Bigtable) — vs hash partitioning.

patterns.name: Lamport Clock
patterns.summary: Scalar logical counter that captures happens-before across processes.

patterns.name: Leader and Followers
patterns.summary: Single leader serializes writes; followers replicate. Foundation of single-leader replication.

patterns.name: Lease
patterns.summary: Time-bounded grant (lock, leadership, cache validity); auto-expires on holder failure.

patterns.name: Low-Water Mark
patterns.summary: Offset below which the log can be safely garbage-collected (Kafka log retention, snapshot in Raft).

patterns.name: Majority Quorum
patterns.summary: Require strict majority for every decision so two disjoint groups can't both progress (Raft, Paxos).

patterns.name: Paxos
patterns.summary: Two-phase consensus (prepare/accept) that tolerates node failures.

patterns.name: Replicated Log
patterns.summary: Identical totally-ordered log on every replica; state machines deterministically derived from it (Raft, Kafka, Bookkeeper).

patterns.name: Request Batch
patterns.summary: Coalesce many small requests into one network round-trip to amortize overhead.

patterns.name: Request Pipeline
patterns.summary: Send next request before previous response arrives; increases throughput on high-latency links.

patterns.name: Request Waiting List
patterns.summary: Server parks a request until conditions (e.g., quorum acks) are met, then responds.

patterns.name: Segmented Log
patterns.summary: Split append-only log into bounded-size segments for easier rotation, deletion, compaction (Kafka, BookKeeper).

patterns.name: Single-Socket Channel
patterns.summary: One TCP connection between two peers carries all messages in order; preserves FIFO.

patterns.name: Singular Update Queue
patterns.summary: Single-threaded event loop serializes updates without locks (LMAX Disruptor, Node.js, Kafka controller).

patterns.name: State Watch
patterns.summary: Clients subscribe to changes on specific keys/paths; server notifies on change (ZooKeeper watches, etcd watches).

patterns.name: Two-Phase Commit
patterns.summary: Coordinator drives prepare + commit phases across all participants. Blocks on coordinator failure.

patterns.name: Version Vector
patterns.summary: Vector of per-node counters attached to values; detects concurrent vs causal updates (Dynamo, Riak).

patterns.name: Versioned Value
patterns.summary: Store every update with a version (MVCC). Enables snapshot reads, conflict detection (Postgres, Spanner, CockroachDB).

patterns.name: Write-Ahead Log
patterns.summary: Persist every state change as an append-only command record before applying. Basis of crash recovery in all serious DBs.

duplicates_already_present: Version Vector ↔ Vector Clock (commonly conflated; technically distinct)
